notes.note: The Ides of May fell on the 15th; this dates the letter to 15 May 51 BC, written as Cicero left Venusia on the first stage of his journey to govern Cilicia.

notes.anchor: the twenty thousand and the eight hundred thousand sesterces
notes.note: A sum of money (HS 20,800, the figures abbreviated in the Latin) that Cicero is pressing Atticus to settle before leaving Rome; the precise transaction is obscure but evidently a debt or purchase Cicero wished wound up in his absence.
